free_text: Scattering of fast neutrons by U-238
Abstract.
Proceedings, paper 26.
inelastic cr.sect. at 90.degr. (prelim.data)
elastic and inelastic cross section
at 90.degr. (prelim.data)
3 MeV IBIS Van de Graaff.
Pulsed proton beam on natural lithium target.
The energy loss in the Li target, usually about 7 keV.
Hollow right cylinder of natural
uranium metal, of 99.27% U-238, 5 cm long,
2.5 cm diam., with a 0.3 cm thick wall.
Time of flight of scattered neutrons.
4 meter flight path.
In these experiments several monitors were used
simultaneously, including a beam current integrator,
long counters and a second time-of-flight system using
a simple proton recoil scintillation counter.
- method of measuring
cross sections in an absolute manner
- Monte Carlo
computer program devised by Parker
10 cm diam. plastic phosphor, 2.5 cm thick,
viewed by two photomultipliers.
- for monitor
Corrected for sample attenuation and multiple
scattering using a Monte-Carlo code by
Parker (see REL-REF).
Principal errors are statistical, from counting
statistics and background correction.
A contribution is also made for uncertainty in
multiple scattering corrections and
accuracy of the relative efficiency of the
scintillation counter.

Data in keV in the article adopted.
The 300 +- 15 keV (6 +) level was very weakly excited.
Four measurements were made of the differential
scattering cross section at 90.degr. at neutron
energies between 845. and 1005. keV, the average value
being 3.8 +- 1.0 mb/sr.
Total error is a combination of the statistical
error and ERR-1 and the total increased by 50%, a
subjectively estimated allowance for other possible
systematic errors.
